title: Starbucks is turning everyday workers into TikTok influencers —changing how brands market to Gen Z
tldr: Starbucks is empowering baristas to create TikTok content, turning frontline employees into authentic brand voices for Gen Z audiences. A concrete case of employee-generated content reshaping brand marketing.
why_fresh: concrete EGC case study reshaping Gen Z marketing
angle: How brands turn frontline staff into their most credible content channel — the playbook and the risks.

title: The Biggest Missed Opportunity in Marketing Isn't AI. It's Your Own Employees
tldr: Argues employees are marketing's most underused asset, more valuable than AI hype. Directly reinforces the case for employee-generated content and advocacy.
angle: Why the human-centered marketing edge is your employees, not your AI stack.

title: LinkedIn is the 'most AI-saturated platform,' new study suggests
tldr: A Pangram study scanning ~1M posts finds LinkedIn is the most AI-generated-content-heavy platform online. A signal about how feeds and media consumption are shifting toward synthetic content.
why_fresh: new study data on platform AI saturation
angle: When feeds go synthetic: what LinkedIn's AI saturation means for trust and human voice in content.

title: Comcast's upcoming split puts spotlight on employee comms amid major change
tldr: As Comcast separates into two public companies, the challenge is giving employees a durable narrative, not a one-off press release. A timely change-management and internal comms case.
angle: How to build an internal narrative that outlasts the announcement during corporate splits.

title: There are millions of undiscovered species on Earth. Vox is trying to find one.
tldr: Vox turns science journalism into an immersive expedition story, discovering a species as a content format. A creative editorial experiment in storytelling and audience engagement.
angle: When the story is the campaign: Vox's expedition journalism as a repeatable engagement model.

title: Mass Content Production Is Quietly Erasing Brand Visibility
tldr: Argues that high-volume, low-distinctiveness content is eroding brand visibility in AI-driven discovery. A counterpoint to scale-at-all-costs content strategies.
angle: The paradox of scale: why producing more content is making brands invisible.

title: Your Best-Ranked Page Might Be Invisible to Google's AI
tldr: Top-10 search rankings no longer guarantee citation in Google's AI Overviews, breaking old SEO assumptions. Essential for rethinking content discoverability.
angle: Ranking without being read: how AI Overviews rewrite the rules of content discoverability.

title: BIRN at 20: how a regional investigative network built trust across the Balkans
tldr: BIRN's two-decade story of building cross-border trust and a sustainable investigative model in a fragmented region. A durable case study in audience trust and media business models.
angle: Trust as a business model: lessons from a 20-year investigative network.

title: Janosch Abel challenges flashy sports photography by immortalising the repetitive labour that truly shapes an athlete
tldr: A photographer swaps glossy sports imagery for the unglamorous grind of training, foregrounding human reality over polish. A visual storytelling counter-trend to hyper-styled content.
angle: People over polish: why unglamorous, real imagery is outperforming perfection.

title: John McCarthy: AI said this column would change the world. A human told me it was crap
tldr: A columnist contrasts AI's flattering feedback with honest human critique, arguing for human judgment in creative work. A sharp, contrarian take on AI in content craft.
angle: AI flatters, humans improve: the case for keeping honest editors in the loop.

title: JAIKU founder Marianne Olaleye on why we shouldn't obsess with Gen Z, and how storytelling can change the world
tldr: The brand strategist behind Glossier and British Library stories argues against Gen Z obsession and for universal storytelling. Practical wisdom on narrative from a working practitioner.
angle: Stop chasing generations: building brand stories on universal human truths.

title: How to rethink the social media marketing funnel with a flywheel
tldr: Argues nonlinear buyer behavior breaks the classic funnel and proposes a flywheel model for social. A useful reframe for omnichannel content planning.
angle: From funnel to flywheel: what nonlinear buying means for content sequencing.

title: 70 Deals & Counting: Creator Economy M&A Sets Record Pace in 1H 2026
tldr: Creator-economy M&A hit a record 70 deals in the first half of 2026, signaling rapid consolidation and maturation. Useful market context for anyone tracking the creator business.
angle: Consolidation wave: what record creator-economy M&A means for independent creators.

title: No, Flock isn't threatening people for debating surveillance
tldr: Surveillance firm Flock's cease-and-desist letter over public criticism became a reputational flashpoint. A live crisis-comms and issue-management lesson in the Streisand effect.
angle: The legal-threat backfire: how heavy-handed responses amplify criticism.
